fact: May 30 is exactly 150 days into the year - that's almost halfway through your entire year of adventures!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for fireflies to start their magical light shows in backyards and forests.
Dandelions are going absolutely crazy right now, shooting their fluffy seed parachutes everywhere like nature's confetti cannons.
The sun rises before 6 AM in most northern places, which means birds start their morning concerts while you're still dreaming.
Baby animals are everywhere! May 30 falls right in the middle of peak birthing season for deer, rabbits, and tons of other mammals.
We're only 3 weeks away from the summer solstice - the longest day of the year in the north!
In Australia and other southern places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves are turning brilliant reds and golds.
Gemini season is in full swing, meaning the constellation Gemini (the twins) is perfectly positioned in the night sky.
Mosquitoes are hatching like crazy around this time because spring rains have created perfect breeding puddles everywhere.
The Northern Hemisphere is tilted so far toward the sun that some places near the Arctic Circle get sunlight for 20+ hours a day!
Tree pollen counts hit their absolute peak around now, making everything look like it's covered in yellow dust.
Ocean temperatures in many places are finally warm enough for sea turtles to start laying their eggs on beaches.
